records.ety: [L. vindicatus, p. p. of vindicare to lay claim to, defend, avenge. See Vengeance.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Vindicated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Vindicating.]

defs: To lay claim to; to assert a right to; to claim.
quotes: Is thine alone the seed that strews the plain? The birds of heaven shall vindicate their grain.

defs: To maintain or defend with success; to prove to be valid; to assert convincingly; to sustain against assault; as, to vindicate a right, claim, or title.
To support or maintain as true or correct, against denial, censure, or objections; to defend; to justify.
quotes: When the respondent denies any proposition, the opponent must directly vindicate . . . that proposition.
Laugh where we must, be candid where we can, But vindicate the ways of God to man.

defs: To maintain, as a law or a cause, by overthrowing enemies.
To liberate; to set free; to deliver.
quotes: I am confident he deserves much more That vindicates his country from a tyrant Than he that saves a citizen.

defs: To avenge; to punish; as, a war to vindicate or punish infidelity.
quotes: God is more powerful to exact subjection and to vindicate rebellion.
